channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: signed.com: because angel investors apparently can't keep track of their investments
item.description: So I recently built Signed, after years of investing in startups as an angel and honestly being a bit flabbergasted at how bad investors are at... doing their job? Signed handles all the document...

item.title: Meet BushJS, a new Nodejs framework
item.description: Welcome to BushJS I'm a Nodejs framework built on top of Express.js and MongoDB. Right now, I'm for APIs only, so you will be using me to develop RESTful APIs, GraphQL, and WebSocket endpoints. I...

item.title: E-Waste Aware — Sanitise First, Then Recycle 🌏
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Earth Day Edition What I Built E-Waste Aware is a Flask web app that guides you through the responsible disposal of old tech — before it ends up in...

item.title: Hidden Answers in Plain Sight: A CLI to Ask the Earth
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Earth Day Edition 🌍 What I Built I built a small command-line experiment called earth-oracle. You ask the Earth a question… and it answers. But not in...

item.title: Kayon for Weekend Challenge: Earth Day Edition
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Earth Day Edition What I Built Kayon is a digital platform that transforms small daily actions into measurable environmental impact. Inspired by the...

item.title: Techn0tz Turns 1 — Help Shape What’s Next
item.description: Techn0tz turns 1 🎉 It started as a simple space to document learning. Over time, it evolved into building real applications in Rust — focusing on how things work, not just how to write them. The...

item.title: Scrub Your Btrfs Before It Scrubs You: Practical `btrfs scrub` + systemd timer
item.description: If you run Btrfs and never schedule btrfs scrub, you are skipping one of the filesystem's most useful maintenance tools. Scrub is not glamorous. It does not make your box faster. It will not clean...

item.title: Day 75 of #100DaysOfCode — Building DevBoard: Job Search and Applications
item.description: Yesterday, employers got their full dashboard. Today, for day 75, I built the candidate side, everything a developer needs to find a job on DevBoard. Browsing listings, searching and filtering by...

item.title: AWS Data & AI Stories #02: Amazon Bedrock Data Automation
item.description: In the first article, I talked about multimodal AI at a high level. Now it is time to go one step deeper. When we say multimodal AI, one of the first real challenges is not the model itself. The...

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36 Drops April 22: What Platform Engineers Actually Need to Know
item.description: TL;DR: Kubernetes v1.36 releases April 22, 2026. The headline features are DRA GPU partitioning, workload-aware preemption for AI/ML jobs, and the permanent removal of the gitRepo volume plugin....

item.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
item.description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

item.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
item.description: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to ... unfriendly states."

item.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
item.description: Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: New Giant Squid Video
item.description: Pretty fantastic video from Japan of a giant squid eating another squid. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog...

item.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
item.description: Tinder users who prove they're a real person by visiting an identity-verifying orb will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb....

item.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
item.description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

item.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
item.description: The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national...

item.title: OpenAI Executive Kevin Weil Is Leaving the Company
item.description: The former Instagram VP is departing the ChatGPT-maker, which is folding the AI science application he led into Codex.

item.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
item.description: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

item.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
item.description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

item.title: Betting on the news raises ethical questions for journalists
item.description: Prediction market exchanges have created an environment where just about any piece of information is potentially monetizable: How well will BTS's new song perform this week? How hot will Los...

item.title: This charming gadget writes bad AI poetry
item.description: I've never been as charmed and frustrated by one gadget as I have with the Poetry Camera. It's a delightful object. White and cherry red with a color-matched woven strap, it looks playful and...

item.title: Gazing Into Sam Altman’s Orb Now Proves You’re Human on Tinder
item.description: Honestly, what’s hotter than a real person?

item.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
Our new favorite budget phones
item.description: Over the last few months, every phone maker under the sun has refreshed its lineups, especially at the affordable end, with no major budget phone launches now expected until next year. That made...

item.title: Tokenmaxxing, OpenAI’s shopping spree, and the AI Anxiety Gap
item.description: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps...

item.title: The best budget smartphones you can buy
item.description: Some of us take a kind of “eat to live” rather than a “live to eat” approach to gadgets. They’re tools that help you get things done, not something you want to invest a lot of time or money in. If...

item.title: Dairy Queen is putting an AI chatbot in its drive-thrus
item.description: Dairy Queen is becoming the latest fast food chain to get in on AI, as it's bringing a chatbot to dozens of its drive-thrus across the US and Canada. It aims to help speed up drive-thru service...

item.title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body
item.description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the tradeoff may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.

item.title: The AirPods Pro 3 are $50 off right now, nearly matching their best-ever price
item.description: From OLED TVs to smartphones, Best Buy’s Upgrade Sale has slashed prices on all kinds of tech, including a wide range of Apple devices. We’ve rounded up the best deals from the sale here, but one...
